en_title: Love, Meditations upon Life and Myself
en_body:
How infinitely terrifying when a person seeks God as the absolute final point opening Hope and the wish for human happiness: only then to lose all Faith at once, and sink into solitude, loneliness revealing in full the solitary truth, that one alone has only oneself, finally and absolutely! Yet one still does not possess oneself as the fulcrum, like the principal fulcrum of Archimedes' lever, to declare, “Give me but one place to stand, and I can move the whole universe!” What that person must understand sooner is that Archimedes did not possess that fulcrum, and the universe around him stood indifferent, towering in its heartlessness, alien to him as it is to that person! Yes! The matter is not that, like Archimedes, one must understand the need to find a fulcrum; bound from childhood to a role and position in society, one cannot easily, as child, grandchild, etc., know oneself and then struggle to become one's own sole and resolute fulcrum!
 Does Love truly exist? Of course it may begin from only one side, but somewhere along the road of knowing, there must be mutual assent and the wish of both to unite! I have passed beyond the game of pursuit, the measuring of another's psychology, or guessing games.. All things concerning Existence are diverse, complex and many-faced: but I choose to simplify the matter, to make it as simple and ordinary as possible. Only I am not an exceptional case; I am one part of the diverse, complex and many-faced living realm; I must live with, possess, and deeply need those involved with or connected by some emotional bond within the life of my human being: human beings diverse, complex and many-faced. And.. in Love, I have no authority to compel the psychological and emotional reasoning of another to bring me the simplest happiness of receiving at once whatever I desire! Therefore, when I, or my psychological and emotional reasoning, make myself and my happiness dependent upon another's will, choice and decision: I no longer possess the fulcrum of the lever to turn the universe of my life, nor complete sovereignty to determine my own happiness!
 Me? I live to seek knowledge and learn how to live most ordinarily with the common limits I share with humankind and my own most personal limits! I can say I cherish you as a precious human being: but distance? “Good intention” is not “good enough”! Meaning that goodwill alone does nothing to enable two sincere human beings to come near each other!

 I see my life as curving lines upon a graph
 The downward and upward with terrifying speed
 While happiness and anguish
 Lie at the final summit of infinite coordinates
 Like the absolute end of heaven and hell.
 I need someone to hold my hand in the dense darkness
 I need someone to descend into hell with me
 So after our hell, together, we shall find
 the heaven within and belonging to each other!
 The Heaven and earth of my Love,
 I need an angel to enter hell with me!
 Two times two is the realm of dream-mist,
 Two times one is every aching pain
 Two beside two are two faces of a coin
 One beside one is release and letting go
 Mathematics is dream and is not real
 Two with one are lonely oddness
 Yet three is Heaven's absolute end
 One waving hand may be calling near
 Two waving hands may be pushing away
 One with two is a game of catch-release
 Nothing at all is true upon this earth
 One squared added to one squared
 When together equal zero, they draw a circle
 Circle is Nothingness, no beginning, no end
 Square exists because four sides wander around
 Square plus square equals Nothingness, a circle
 Every theorem bears a sign around it
 Finding nothing means going round and round
 Life's equation rises, falls in sorrow, joy!
note: The prose invokes Archimedes' lever and fulcrum, while the closing geometry turns the circle and zero into Buddhist Nothingness without beginning or end.
